poster_title: ESSAYBENCH: Evaluating Large Language Models in Multi-Genre Chinese Essay Writing
authors: Fan Gao1, Dongyuan Li1, Ding Xia1, Fei Mi2, Yasheng Wang2, Lifeng Shang2, Baojun Wang2

content: Current evaluation methods for LLM-generated essays are coarse-grained.
Chinese essays have unique characteristics and genre-specific criteria.
Different genres of Chinese essays have distinct requirements.
Existing frameworks fail to capture nuanced demands of Chinese essay writing.
Lack of a robust evaluation framework limits LLMs in educational settings.
Prompts categorized into Open-Ended and Constrained sets.
Proposed fine-grained and genre-specific evaluation framework.
panels.figure_caption: Overview of the ESSAYBENCH dataset curation, representative prompts, and the evaluation framework.

content: Human agreement study and quality sensitivity analysis.
Benchmarking 15 large-scale LLMs on Chinese essay writing.
Evaluation protocol aligns closely with human judgments.
Significant improvement in distinguishing essay quality.
panels.figure_caption: Comparison between coarse-grained evaluation and the proposed protocol.

content: Performance varies across different prompt sets.
LLMs show strengths and weaknesses in specific genres.
ESSAYBENCH is a valuable tool for evaluating LLM-generated Chinese essays.
Proposed evaluation protocol enhances ability to distinguish essay quality.

Fig1.caption: Comparison between coarse-grained evaluation and the proposed protocol.
Fig1.description: 图1展示了粗粒度评估的比较结果。

Fig2.caption: Overview of the ESSAYBENCH dataset curation, representative prompts, and the evaluation framework.
Fig2.description: 图2概述了ESSAYBENCH数据集的构建、代表性提示以及评估框架。

Fig4.caption: Comparison of Performance by Sets.
Fig4.description: 图4展示了不同集合的性能比较。
